name: Autism Brain Imaging Data Exchange (ABIDE I)
description: A large multi-site aggregation of resting-state fMRI and structural MRI from 1,112 individuals — 539 with autism spectrum disorder and 573 typical controls — with accompanying phenotypic data. Because autism centrally involves social cognition and social interaction, ABIDE is a heavily used resource for studying the neural basis of social-brain differences and for building predictive models of social-cognitive phenotypes.
citation: Di Martino A, Yan C-G, Li Q, et al. (2014). The autism brain imaging data exchange: towards a large-scale evaluation of the intrinsic brain architecture in autism. Molecular Psychiatry, 19(6), 659-667.
